section_label: NICU batch 3 — supportive, antifungals, anticoagulants, endocrine, sedation
review_disclaimer: DRAFT — pending clinician review. Verify every dose against your local protocol and current vial concentration before administration.

drugs.monitoring: Electrolytes (Na, K, Cl, HCO₃), urine output.
drugs.adverse_effects: Metabolic acidosis, hypokalaemia, hyponatraemia, sulfa hypersensitivity, blood dyscrasias.
drugs.contraindications: Sulfonamide hypersensitivity, hyperchloraemic acidosis, hyponatraemia, hypokalaemia, severe renal/hepatic impairment.

review.notes: Acetazolamide + furosemide combo for PHVD is now AVOIDED — Kennedy 2001 RCT showed worse outcomes.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: Then 50 mg/kg over 4 h, then 100 mg/kg over 16 h
doses.comments: Total 300 mg/kg over 21 h. Dilute in D5W (100 mL for ≤ 5 kg, scale up). Anaphylactoid reactions risk during the load.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 1–2 mL of 10% solution (or 0.5–1 mL of 20%) every 6 hours

drugs.monitoring: LFTs, INR, paracetamol level (timed from ingestion), creatinine.
drugs.adverse_effects: Anaphylactoid reactions (flushing, urticaria, bronchospasm, hypotension) in 10–20% during IV load — slow infusion, antihistamines.

india_formulations.notes: Avoid 20-mL multidose bottles in neonates — benzalkonium chloride may worsen bronchospasm.

doses.comments: Use other K-lowering measures if K > 7.5 mmol/L.

drugs.monitoring: HR (tachycardia), tremor, K (transient drop), glucose (transient rise).
drugs.adverse_effects: Tachycardia, tremor, hypokalaemia, hyperglycaemia, agitation.

doses.frequency: every 8–12 hours (start 8–12 h after the load)
doses.comments: Aminophylline is 80% theophylline by weight. Equivalent oral theophylline dose = aminophylline dose × 0.8.

drugs.monitoring: Therapeutic theophylline 5–12 mcg/mL (apnoea), 10–20 (bronchodilation). HR (tachycardia), feeding intolerance, glucose.
drugs.adverse_effects: Tachycardia, irritability, vomiting, feeding intolerance, hyperglycaemia, seizure (toxic levels), GI bleeding.

review.notes: Caffeine is preferred — wider therapeutic index, less monitoring.

doses.indication: Invasive Candida (alternative to fluconazole / conventional ampho)
doses.duration: ≥ 2 weeks after blood culture clearance + symptom resolution

drugs.monitoring: Renal function, K, Mg, LFTs, CBC.
drugs.adverse_effects: Less nephrotoxic than conventional amphotericin; infusion reactions (fever, chills), hypokalaemia, hypomagnesaemia, transaminitis.

drugs.monitoring: Renal function, K, Mg, LFTs, CBC.
drugs.adverse_effects: Less nephrotoxic than conventional ampho. Infusion reactions, electrolyte losses, hepatotoxicity.

review.notes: Liposomal preferred over conventional / lipid-complex when renal function is precarious.

drugs.monitoring: Daily renal function (Cr), K, Mg, LFTs, CBC.
drugs.adverse_effects: Nephrotoxicity (high), infusion reactions (fever, chills, rigors), hypokalaemia, hypomagnesaemia, anaemia, transaminitis, phlebitis.
drugs.incompatibilities: Do NOT mix with NS (precipitates) — use D5W only.

review.notes: Lipid formulations preferred when affordable due to better tolerability.

drugs.monitoring: Watch for hypertrophic pyloric stenosis (esp. < 6 weeks), QT prolongation if comorbidities.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypertrophic pyloric stenosis (KIDs list — risk in neonates), QT prolongation, cholestatic hepatitis, pseudomembranous colitis.
drugs.contraindications: Macrolide hypersensitivity, history of cholestatic jaundice with macrolides.

review.notes: KIDs list — avoid in neonates UNLESS specific indication (pertussis / chlamydia / ureaplasma).

drugs.monitoring: Clinical response; CBC, RFT.
drugs.adverse_effects: Rash, eosinophilia, transaminitis, neutropenia.
drugs.contraindications: Aztreonam hypersensitivity (true cross-reactivity with penicillins is minimal).

review.notes: Best option for gram-negative coverage in true severe penicillin allergy.

doses.frequency: every 24 hours in first 2 months of life, then every 12 hours

doses.ga_band: Preterm ≥ 34 weeks and term, first month
doses.frequency: every 24 hours, then every 12 hours after 1 month

drugs.monitoring: Electrolytes, urine output, weight, hearing if prolonged use.
drugs.adverse_effects: Similar to furosemide but less ototoxicity; hypokalaemia, hyponatraemia, hypochloraemia, hypocalcaemia, hypomagnesaemia.

review.notes: 1 mg bumetanide ≈ 40 mg furosemide.

drugs.category: Partial opioid agonist (μ partial agonist / κ antagonist)

doses.indication: Neonatal Abstinence Syndrome (NAS) — adjunct or first-line where available
doses.loading_dose_per_kg: Initial 15.9 mcg/kg/day SL in 3 divided doses
doses.comments: Titrate up in 25% increments (max 6 up-titrations). Wean by 10% of stabilisation dose until 10% of initial, then stop.

drugs.monitoring: Finnegan / NAS score, RR, SpO₂, feeding pattern, weight.
drugs.adverse_effects: Sedation, respiratory depression (less than full agonists), constipation.

cross_checks.source: Kraft 2017 NEJM (BBORN trial) — buprenorphine SL vs morphine for NAS

review.notes: Sublingual buprenorphine is shorter NICU stay vs oral morphine in NAS.

doses.indication: Cardiac arrest with documented hypoCa / hyperK / hyperMg / CCB overdose
doses.route: IV slow infusion (over 30–60 min ; CENTRAL line preferred)

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 33 mg of CaCl 10% per 100 mL citrated blood (= 0.33 mL per 100 mL)

drugs.monitoring: ECG (arrhythmia), IV site (severe necrosis on extravasation), serum ionised Ca.
drugs.adverse_effects: Bradycardia, hypotension if rapid; severe tissue necrosis / sloughing if extravasates.
drugs.contraindications: Hypercalcaemia, ventricular fibrillation (relative — only if hypoCa documented), digitalis toxicity.
drugs.incompatibilities: Do NOT mix with bicarbonate or phosphate.

review.notes: 10% CaCl = 100 mg/mL salt = 27 mg/mL elemental Ca = 1.4 mEq/mL elemental Ca. Calcium gluconate generally preferred due to lower acidosis risk.

doses.comments: Heart failure: 0.4–1.6 mg/kg/day divided every 8 hours. Reduce in renal impairment.

drugs.monitoring: BP, K, Cr (rise > 30% prompts dose review), urine output.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypotension (especially first dose), hyperkalaemia, AKI / oliguria, neutropenia, cough (rare in infants).
drugs.contraindications: Bilateral renal artery stenosis, anuria, prior angioedema.

review.notes: Use with extreme care in preterm — significant Cr drop reported. Always start with a test dose.

drugs.monitoring: LFTs, CBC, electrolytes.
drugs.adverse_effects: Transaminitis, infusion reactions (rare in neonates), histamine-mediated flushing if rapid.

cross_checks.agreement: agrees on m²-based dosing in neonates

review.notes: Body surface area: BSA(m²) = √[(wt × ht)/3600] ≈ ~0.2–0.25 for term neonate.

drugs.monitoring: Clinical response; CBC, RFT.
drugs.adverse_effects: Same as other cephalosporins.

doses.indication: Severe gram-negative infection (with level monitoring only)

drugs.monitoring: Therapeutic peak 15–25 mcg/mL; trough 5–10 mcg/mL. CBC weekly (aplastic anaemia).
drugs.adverse_effects: Grey baby syndrome (cardiovascular collapse, cyanosis, hypothermia, abdominal distension), aplastic anaemia (idiosyncratic), bone marrow suppression (dose-related).
drugs.contraindications: Use without serum monitoring in neonates (KIDs list), prior aplastic anaemia.
drugs.black_box_warning: Serious and fatal blood dyscrasias (aplastic anaemia, hypoplastic anaemia, thrombocytopenia, granulocytopenia) have occurred — use only when other antibiotics are contraindicated or ineffective.

review.notes: Last-resort drug in modern NICU; almost never used in developed centres.

doses.comments: Administer with food (improves absorption). IV not recommended due to lack of data.

drugs.monitoring: Electrolytes (Na, K, Cl), urine output.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypokalaemia, hyponatraemia, hypochloraemic alkalosis, hypercalcaemia, hyperuricaemia.

review.notes: Often used in combination with spironolactone for BPD.

india_formulations.strength: 200 mg, 400 mg tab; 200 mg/5 mL syrup

drugs.monitoring: RFT (renal clearance), drug interactions.
drugs.adverse_effects: Many drug interactions (CYP inhibition), bradycardia (with rapid IV), thrombocytopenia, gynaecomastia, transaminitis.

review.notes: Largely replaced by ranitidine and famotidine due to drug interactions; ranitidine itself withdrawn over NDMA — most NICU use famotidine now.

doses.comments: Titrate to NAS scoring; discontinue gradually based on stability.

drugs.monitoring: HR, BP, sedation level.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypotension, bradycardia, sedation. Rebound hypertension on abrupt withdrawal.

review.notes: Adjunct only — primary NAS treatment is morphine or buprenorphine.

doses.indication: Facilitation of extubation / BPD prevention (DART low-dose)
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: Day 1–3: 0.075 mg/kg q12h ; Day 4–6: 0.05 mg/kg q12h ; Day 7–8: 0.025 mg/kg q12h ; Day 9–10: 0.01 mg/kg q12h
doses.comments: DART regimen — total course 10 days. Discuss benefits vs neurodevelopmental risks with family.

drugs.monitoring: Glucose, BP, growth, infection signs, GI bleeding signs, CNS exam (neurodevelopmental risk in preterm).
drugs.adverse_effects: Hyperglycaemia, hypertension, GI perforation, growth suppression, adrenal suppression, increased infection risk, neurodevelopmental impairment (high-dose, early treatment), HCM (chronic high dose).
drugs.contraindications: Active untreated infection (sepsis), early use (< 1 week PNA, high dose) — increased CP risk.

review.notes: Avoid early high-dose dexamethasone (< 1 week PNA, > 0.5 mg/kg/day) — neurodevelopmental risk. Hydrocortisone is alternative for early adrenal insufficiency.

doses.comments: Adjust to glucose targets. If no response in 2–3 weeks, discontinue.

drugs.monitoring: Blood glucose, BP, fluid balance, CBC, electrolytes.
drugs.adverse_effects: Fluid retention (often co-administered with chlorothiazide), hypertrichosis, hyperuricaemia, neutropenia, pulmonary hypertension (case reports), GI upset.
drugs.contraindications: Hypersensitivity (often cross-reactive with sulfa); thiazide allergy (relative).

review.notes: Co-administer chlorothiazide for fluid retention.

doses.indication: CHF / SVT / atrial flutter — total digitalising dose (TDD)
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: TDD 15–25 mcg/kg IV (or 20–30 mcg/kg PO); divided 50% / 25% / 25% over 24 h

drugs.monitoring: ECG (PR prolongation, AV block, junctional rhythm), serum digoxin level (target 0.5–2 ng/mL), K, Mg, Ca, RFT.
drugs.adverse_effects: Toxicity — bradycardia, AV block, vomiting, lethargy, ventricular arrhythmia. Hypokalaemia and hypomagnesaemia potentiate toxicity.
drugs.contraindications: VF, idiopathic hypertrophic subaortic stenosis, AV block (without pacemaker), digitalis intoxication.
drugs.incompatibilities: Many — give IV slowly over 5 min.

review.notes: IV TDD = 75% of oral TDD. Very narrow therapeutic index — measure level at 6 h post-dose.

doses.comments: Adjust to anti-Xa 0.5–1.0 U/mL (4 h post-dose).

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 2 mg/kg/dose initial (range 0.8–3 mg/kg q12h to maintain target)

drugs.monitoring: Anti-factor Xa level 4 h post-dose ; CBC; signs of bleeding; renal function.
drugs.adverse_effects: Bleeding, heparin-induced thrombocytopenia (HIT — rare with LMWH vs UFH), hyperkalaemia, transaminitis, injection-site haematoma.
drugs.contraindications: Active major bleeding, severe thrombocytopenia, heparin / pork hypersensitivity, recent CNS / ocular surgery, recent IVH.

review.notes: Always evaluate for IVH before starting in preterm. PT, aPTT, platelets, renal function at baseline.

doses.route: IV (or IM in adults; IV preferred in paediatrics)
doses.comments: Dosed in PHENYTOIN EQUIVALENTS (PE). 1 mg PE = 1 mg phenytoin sodium.

drugs.monitoring: Continuous ECG + BP during loading. Free phenytoin level (target 1–2 mcg/mL).
drugs.adverse_effects: Same as phenytoin (cardiac, dermatologic, hepatic, blood dyscrasia) BUT less infusion-site phlebitis / extravasation injury. Pruritus / paraesthesia at infusion sites is common.

review.notes: Always order in PE. Compatible with D5W and NS — unlike phenytoin.

drugs.category: Anticoagulant — direct thrombin / factor Xa via antithrombin

doses.comments: Continuous infusion thereafter, target ACT 180–220 s.
Target anti-Xa 0.35–0.7 U/mL (or aPTT 60–85 s). Treatment course 10–14 days; consider switch to LMWH after 3–5 days. Renal vein thrombosis: 6 weeks–3 months total.

doses.indication: Maintaining patency of central vascular catheters

drugs.monitoring: ACT (ECMO), aPTT or anti-Xa level, platelet count (HIT screen at day 4 and weekly), bleeding signs, IVH on cranial USG (preterm).
drugs.adverse_effects: Bleeding, heparin-induced thrombocytopenia (HIT, type II — rare in neonates), hyperkalaemia, transaminitis, osteoporosis (chronic).
drugs.contraindications: Active major bleeding, severe thrombocytopenia, recent IVH, recent CNS surgery.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: Physiologic 7–9 mg/m²/day in 2–3 doses ; salt-losing CAH may need higher mineralocorticoid (fludrocortisone)
doses.frequency: Adjust based on growth, electrolytes, BP

doses.indication: Refractory hypotension (vasopressor-resistant) / suspected adrenal insufficiency in shock
doses.comments: Use lowest effective dose; taper to minimise HPA suppression.

doses.indication: BPD prevention (alternative to dexamethasone, low-risk approach)
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 1 mg/kg/dose every 12 h × 7 d, then 0.5 mg/kg/dose every 12 h × 3 d (PREMILOC regimen)
doses.comments: 1 mg/kg/dose total daily for 7 days, taper × 3 days. PREMILOC trial showed BPD reduction without neurodevelopmental impairment.

drugs.monitoring: Glucose, BP, Na, K, growth.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hyperglycaemia, hypertension, GI perforation (especially with concurrent indomethacin / ibuprofen — separate by 24 h), HPA-axis suppression.

review.notes: Hydrocortisone preferred over dexamethasone for early BPD prevention in extreme preterm — fewer neurodevelopmental concerns.

doses.comments: Crush tablet, mix with small volume of water/breast milk. Do NOT mix with soy formula or iron — reduces absorption.

drugs.monitoring: TSH and free T4 at 2 weeks, then every 1–3 months for first year. Target free T4 in upper half of reference range, TSH 0.5–2 mU/L.
drugs.adverse_effects: Tachycardia, irritability, poor weight gain (overdose); persistent symptoms, lethargy (underdose). Craniosynostosis (chronic overdose).

cross_checks.source: AAP / Indian Society of Paediatric Endocrinology

review.notes: Always start as soon as congenital hypothyroidism is confirmed by repeat TSH. Treatment delay > 2 weeks risks neurodevelopmental impairment.
